papers.title: Technical Trading Strategies And the effect of trigger indicators
papers.summary: This study evaluates moving average strategies combined with RSI and MACD on the OMX Stockholm 30 index from 2000–2011. Results show no statistically significant excess returns compared to buy-and-hold, even with trigger indicators. The findings highlight the impact of transaction costs and question the robustness of technical trading profitability.
key_findings: Dual moving average strategies do not generate statistically significant excess returns
Adding RSI and MACD as trigger indicators does not significantly improve performance
Transaction costs eliminate much of the apparent profitability of technical strategies
works_best_when: long time series with potential weak inefficiencies
implementation_notes: Maps to trend_follower strategies using moving average crossovers
Acts as a negative benchmark to validate strategy robustness
Useful for risk validation layer in strategy generation agent
papers.backtestable_reason: Study evaluates combinations and statistical significance rather than defining a single executable trading rule

papers.title: The best combination of indicators for the U.S. stock market
papers.summary: This study evaluates 1,440 combinations of technical indicators including MA, RSI, and Bollinger Bands across U.S. stocks. It analyzes performance metrics such as return, drawdown, and trading frequency, and explores integration with deep learning models. Results highlight that combinations of indicators outperform single indicators and can inform feature selection.
key_findings: Indicator combinations outperform single indicators in many scenarios
Different combinations optimize for different metrics like return or drawdown
Technical indicators can serve as effective features for deep learning models
fails_when: overfitting in deep learning without feature selection
implementation_notes: Supports building multi-indicator strategy generation agent
Suggests using auxiliary vs primary signal separation
Not directly executable without selecting specific rule combinations
papers.backtestable_reason: Defines a framework for testing many indicator combinations rather than a single concrete trading strategy

papers.title: A Comprehensive Analysis of a 4 EMA Trading Strategy
papers.summary: This paper presents a trend-following strategy using four EMAs of different periods to identify market direction and generate trading signals. Crossovers between short-term and long-term EMAs indicate entry and exit points, while alignment confirms trend strength. The strategy is effective in trending markets but prone to false signals in sideways conditions.
key_findings: Multiple EMA alignment improves trend confirmation and signal reliability
Short-term vs long-term EMA crossovers provide entry and exit signals
Strategy performs best in trending markets and poorly in range-bound markets
implementation_notes: Direct extension of EMA crossover trend_follower strategies
Can be simplified into dual EMA crossover for current engine
Multi-EMA alignment can be approximated with additional filters
papers.backtestable_reason: Can be reduced to EMA crossover logic supported by existing trend_follower framework

papers.title: The Comparative Effectiveness of RSI and MACD Indicators in Managing Stock Price Volatility of Indonesian State-Owned Banks in 2024
papers.summary: This study analyzes RSI and MACD as tools for explaining stock price volatility in Indonesian state-owned banks. Both indicators significantly influence price movements, with RSI capturing overbought/oversold conditions and MACD identifying trend momentum. Their combined use provides stronger explanatory power than individual indicators.
key_findings: RSI effectively identifies overbought and oversold conditions
MACD captures trend direction and momentum reliably
Combining RSI and MACD improves explanatory power for price movements
implementation_notes: Requires integration logic beyond current single-strategy engine
papers.backtestable_reason: Focuses on explanatory regression analysis rather than explicit trading rules

papers.title: Generating Alpha: A Hybrid AI-Driven Trading System Integrating Technical Analysis, Machine Learning and Financial Sentiment for Regime-Adaptive Equity Strategies
papers.summary: This paper proposes a hybrid trading system combining technical indicators (EMA, MACD, RSI, Bollinger Bands), machine learning, sentiment analysis, and regime detection. The system dynamically adapts to market conditions and outperforms benchmark indices with improved risk-adjusted returns. It highlights the limitations of standalone strategies and the benefits of multi-modal integration.
key_findings: Hybrid systems outperform standalone technical strategies
Regime detection improves adaptability across market conditions
Sentiment analysis reduces downside risk during adverse events
implementation_notes: Future direction for multi-agent strategy generation system
Combines trend_follower and mean_reversion with external signals
Requires significant extension beyond current backtesting engine
papers.backtestable_reason: Requires ML models, sentiment data, and regime detection not supported in current engine
